steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to contrast the locations of the two specimens. The correct choice must name both locations and use language that highlights the difference between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Like Mason, Elliot is a *Triceratops* fossil specimen that lived in the Late Cretaceous period.’: emphasizes similarity rather than contrast between the locations. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Mason is housed at the Museum of Science in Boston, Massachusetts, while Elliot is housed at the National Museum of Nature and Science in Tokyo, Japan.’: names both locations (Boston and Tokyo) and uses ‘while’ to signal the contrast. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The National Museum of Nature and Science in Tokyo, Japan, houses Elliot, a *Triceratops* fossil specimen.’: describes only one location, so no contrast is drawn. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The *Triceratops* fossil specimen Mason is not the only such specimen currently housed in a museum or institute.’: ignores location entirely. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
